    "Activity H (HVAC rough) has a duration of 2 days and starts on day 22, finishing on day 24.",
    "Activities F (Electrical rough) and G (Plumbing top-out) both have durations of 4 days, starting on day 22 and finishing on day 26.",
    "The successor to F, G, and H is Activity I (Rough inspection), which cannot start until all three are complete (day 26).",
    "Adding 1 extra workday of rework to Activity H increases its duration from 2 days to 3 days, meaning it finishes on day 25 instead of day 24."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Proceed with the planned schedule without modification to the critical path.",
    "Absorb the 1-day delay of Activity H within its 2 days of available float."
  ],
  "rationale": "Activity H has 2 days of total float because it is scheduled in parallel with Activities F and G, which both take 4 days. Since the rough inspection (Activity I) depends on the completion of F, G, and H, the path through H is not critical. Increasing H's duration by 1 workday (from 2 to 3 days) does not exceed the 4-day controlling duration of F and G, resulting in 0 days of delay to the project finish date.",
  "references": [
    "CPM Scheduling Standards"
  ]
}
